title: Tesla Q4 2025 and Full Year 2025 Vehicle Production, Deliveries and Deployments
content: AUSTIN, Texas, January 2, 2026 — Tesla, Inc. announced Q4 2025 production and delivery numbers. In Q4 2025, Tesla produced 459,445 vehicles and delivered 495,570 vehicles, up 8% year-over-year. Full-year 2025 deliveries were 1.79 million, down 2% from 2024 due to a weak H1 caused by the Model Y refresh factory downtime. Model Y was the best-selling BEV globally for the third consecutive year. Model 3 and Model Y together accounted for 94% of deliveries. Energy storage deployments in Q4 2025 were 11.0 GWh, up 243% year-over-year, bringing the full-year 2025 total to 31.4 GWh; Megapack is now Tesla's second most profitable product line. On January 29, 2026, Tesla reported Q4 2025 financial results: revenue of $25.7 billion (+2% YoY), automotive gross margin of 17.0%, and operating income of $1.7 billion. Net income was $2.3 billion. Elon Musk, CEO, said on the call: 'We expect vehicle growth of 20-30% in 2026 driven by the new affordable Model Y launch and robotaxi expansion. Energy storage is becoming a dominant part of our business.' The new lower-cost Model Y launched in the US on March 1, 2026, priced at $34,990. Tesla Supercharger network reached 65,000 stalls at 7,100 stations globally at year-end 2025. FSD (Full Self-Driving) subscriber base grew to 2.8 million active users in December 2025. Tesla ended 2025 with $36.6 billion in cash and equivalents.
